narrative: FY2024-25 is the primary benchmark year. Tata is strongest on channel reach and procurement transparency: it reports 1,661 dealers/distributors, 38% sales through dealers/distributors, and zero trading houses for purchases. JSW and Jindal lead the two product-information indicators at 100% each, while Tata remains materially behind at 52% for environmental/social product information and 17% for recycling/safe-disposal information. SAIL’s product-information indicators fall to 0%, but it retains zero trading-house dependence and reports 168 dealers/distributors. Jindal is the most improved integrated product-responsibility performer, combining 100% product-information coverage with 367 dealers/distributors and strong stainless-specific circularity claims, but it remains the weakest on trading-house dependence: 478 trading houses, 80% purchases from trading houses, and 62% top-10 trading-house concentration. For Tata senior management, the key gap is not circular-economy capability—Tata recycled about 4.2 Mt scrap in FY2024-25—but translation of that capability into full turnover-linked consumer/product information coverage.
